theme: How to choose the right destination for your corporate event

linkedin.text:
The most beautiful destination is almost never the right one. Here's the framework we use instead.

After 200+ events in 20+ countries, this is the decision tree behind every recommendation we make:

1️⃣ Define the objective → reward trip, team-building, or conference-plus-leisure? The criteria change completely with purpose.

2️⃣ Set the real budget → not "what do you want to spend," but "what is each person worth to you?" Work backwards from value, not cost.

3️⃣ Match destination to brand → Luxury: Monaco, Amalfi. Tech: Berlin, Copenhagen. Family culture: Lisbon, Seville. Adventure: Iceland, Oman.

4️⃣ Check logistics → flight time (under 4h ideal), visas, season. Avoid school-holiday spikes for European venues.

5️⃣ Validate with real data → a 9.4 from a 60-person pharma group in Florence beats any brochure.

The right destination isn't the prettiest. It's the one that best serves your specific group's purpose.

💬 Comment "FRAMEWORK" and we'll send the full destination-fit checklist.

#CorporateEvents #IncentiveTravel #EmployeeEngagement #B2BEvents #MICE #Leadership

instagram.text:
The prettiest destination is rarely the right one. 🗺️

Our 5-step framework after 200+ events:

1️⃣ Define the objective (reward vs. team building)
2️⃣ Set the real budget (per-person total value)
3️⃣ Match destination to brand culture
4️⃣ Check logistics (flight time, visas, season)
5️⃣ Validate with past-group data

The right destination serves your group's purpose — not just the grid 😉

facebook.text:
Most companies pick a destination because someone on the board loved it on holiday. That's how trips go wrong. 🗺️

After 200+ events, we've learned the right destination has almost nothing to do with how pretty it is.

We start with the objective — is this a reward for the top performers, or team-building for everyone? Then the real budget: not what you want to spend, but what each person is worth to you. Then we match the place to your brand, check the logistics, and validate it against real feedback from similar groups.

The prettiest place isn't the answer. The right fit is.

Where did your last company trip go — and did it actually fit the team? Tell us in the comments. 💬
